title: Teucrium chowii
intro: Teucrium chamaedrys, also known as Wall Germander, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Wall Germander include flavonoids, diterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Wall Germander is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
